response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems developer, but the profile is a poor match for this embedded networking security role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source-code modification experience, and hands-on knowledge of L2/L3 networking and security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). The candidate's career is centered on web/product development in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with only brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and no listed Japanese proficiency, kernel work, or switch/security-protocol experience.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for the role
Minimal C/UNIX and no Linux kernel modification or embedded firmware experience
No demonstrated experience with L2/L3 switching, MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF, or related security technologies

key_factors.factor: Lack of C/Linux kernel and embedded networking security experience
Solid general engineering and systems background
